title: Depth-First Search, Recursion, Paths, and Backtracking · Independent Program Trial
summary: An independent accessibility club receives a different grid, seed, algorithm, input profile, display scale, assistance need, platform, and failure condition and transfers depth-first search, recursion, paths, and backtracking into a changed traceable DFS solver with path witness. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of traceable DFS solver with path witness with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pract…
objectives: Define start, goal, passable-neighbor order, visited policy, result, and failure contract before traversal.
Mark a vertex when discovered so cycles and converging paths cannot schedule repeated work.
Separate explored vertices, active route, rejected branches, and final start-to-goal witness.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Dfs Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: maze-dfs-evidence-1trcdc" that uses case 3950 to validate DFS visitation, backtracking, route witness, termination, and depth limits. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import or claim native tkinter, Tcl, Tk, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, or packaged release behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, recorded Tcl and Tk patch levels, native widget and display tests, keyboard and assistive-technology playtests, representative profiles, clean platform-native PyInstaller 6.21 builds, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Dfs Evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Dfs Evidence into observable performance evidence. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Dfs Evidence, keep Dfs Evidence connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-dfs-evidence-1trcdc
# Competency: maze-dfs-evidence.
# Case 3950: a clean desktop release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: resource use must remain below an explicit budget.
# Changed case: replace pointer input with a single-action keyboard path.
def evidence_maze_dfs_evidence_1trcdc(path_steps: int = 31, valid_steps: int = 31, explored: int = 42, repeated_vertices: int = 0, recursion_safe: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1trcdc = "1trcdc-cdcrt1-3950"
    assert evidence_variant_1trcdc
    evidence_axes_1trcdc = (
        "grid1trcdc",
        "topology3950",
        "searchcdcrt1",
        "event1trcdc3950",
        "access39501trcdc",
        "recoverycdcrt13950",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1trcdc)) == 6
    if path_steps < 1 or valid_steps < 0 or explored < path_steps:
        return False, valid_steps, "dfs-count"
    if valid_steps != path_steps:
        return False, valid_steps, "invalid-path-step"
    if repeated_vertices > 0:
        return False, repeated_vertices, "visited-policy"
    if not recursion_safe:
        return False, explored, "recursion-limit"
    assert valid_steps == path_steps
    return True, path_steps, "valid-dfs-route"
